ogTitle: Thevenard vs Thomas Plain: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Thevenard and Thomas Plain.
heading: Thevenard vs Thomas Plain — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Thevenard, South Australia and Thomas Plain, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Thevenard has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Thomas Plain (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Thevenard or Thomas Plain safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Thevenard has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Thomas Plain scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Thevenard scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Thomas Plain scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Thevenard or Thomas Plain?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Thevenard has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
